rxn_class_summary: Anodic oxidation of methanol at a boron-doped diamond (BDD) electrode generates methoxy radicals, which enable construction of bisdimethyl acetals from dimethoxybenzyl ethers and an electrochemical biomimetic synthesis of the benzofuran neolignan (±)-licarin A from isoeugenol.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation: MeOH/MeO- is oxidized at the BDD anode to generate methoxy radicals (MeO•); substrates (electron-rich arenes, isoeugenol) are also oxidized at the anode to aryl radical cations under CPE conditions.

key_intermediates.name: oxocarbenium-type cation (B) en route to bisdimethyl acetal 2
phenoxyl/benzylic radical cation D from a phenol precursor
oxocarbenium E leading to aldehyde 3 by loss of MeOR

electron_flow: At the anode, methanol/methoxide is oxidized by single-electron transfer to give methoxy radicals; in parallel, electron-rich arene substrates undergo anodic SET to radical cations that are trapped by MeOH/MeO-, followed by a second oxidation and loss of a proton or MeOR to deliver bisdimethyl acetals, aldehydes, or (for isoeugenol) radical intermediate 9 that couples/cyclizes to licarin A.

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline N-oxide)
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis of MeOH in the presence of DMPO gave an ESR signal matching the DMPO O-methoxy adduct (A_N = 13.37 G, A_H = 7.98 G), confirming generation of methoxy (not hydroxymethyl) radicals; signal intensity followed BDD > Pt > GC anode.

probe_reagents.name: Linear sweep voltammetry of isoeugenol (5)
probe_reagents.observation: Established first and second oxidation potentials used to set CPE potentials (entries 1–6); higher potential on BDD (1.06 V vs SCE) gave the best licarin A yield (40%).

probe_reagents.name: Anode material variation (BDD vs Pt vs GC)
probe_reagents.observation: BDD anode gave the strongest methoxy-radical ESR signal and the highest yield of licarin A (40%), consistent with more efficient MeO• generation at BDD.

notes: The mechanism is supported chiefly by DMPO spin-trapping/ESR detection of methoxy radicals and by anode-material-dependent yields, plus LSV measurements of substrate oxidation potentials; no KIE, DFT, or isotope labeling is reported. Two parallel anodic pathways are proposed: (i) direct SET oxidation of the arene to a radical cation followed by MeOH/MeO- addition (Scheme 1, intermediates A→C→B→2 and D→E→3), and (ii) anodic generation of MeO• that can add to the arene; both can plausibly contribute. For isoeugenol, vicinal byproducts 7 and 8 are rationalized via self-coupling of radical intermediate 9.
